summary: Iteration 27 - Enterprise Network Topology with LLDP/MAC Integration. All backend (21/21) and frontend tests passed. Topology correctly shows 6-layer hierarchy: Internet -> Zyxel Firewall -> HPE Core -> Netgear Distribution -> 4 Access Switches -> iLO. LLDP edges connect Zyxel->HPE and HPE->Netgear with port labels. MAC edges show 10G connections on Port 9 and Port 10. Inferred edges for .6 and .7 have '10G Uplink' labels.

backend_topology_hierarchy: HPE Core Switch at layer 2 (role=core_switch, name contains 'Armadio')
Netgear Distribution at layer 3 (role=distribution_switch, name contains 'Armadio')
backend_lldp_edges: LLDP edge Zyxel->HPE with label 'LAN1 <-> Port 1' (source=lldp)
LLDP edge HPE->Netgear with label 'Port 1 <-> Port 24' (source=lldp)
backend_mac_edges: MAC edge Port 9 with '10G' label (from=192.168.1.3, to=192.168.1.4, type=trunk)
MAC edge Port 10 with '10G' label (from=192.168.1.3, to=192.168.1.5, type=trunk)
backend_inferred_edges: Inferred edge to 192.168.1.6 with label '10G Uplink' (type=trunk)
Inferred edge to 192.168.1.7 with label '10G Uplink' (type=trunk)
frontend_network_map: Navigate to Stato Rete -> Mappa tab works
React Flow canvas renders with 9 nodes
Hierarchical layout visible: Internet -> Firewall -> Core -> Distribution -> Access -> iLO
frontend_toolbar_buttons: Modifica button toggles connect mode (shows 'Modifica ON')
Salva Layout button saves layout (toast: 'Layout salvato con successo!')
frontend_legend: Legend shows: WAN, Trunk, Accesso, Server, MGMT, LLDP, Manuale

critical_code_review_comments: Topology inference engine correctly classifies devices by type and role
LLDP edges properly replace inferred edges using set-based overlap detection
MAC edges include 10G speed labels from port_speeds collection
Inferred edges for 10G switches correctly labeled based on device name hints
Frontend React Flow v12 implementation renders hierarchical layout correctly
All interactive elements have proper data-testid attributes
seed_data_creation: No new seed data - used existing 86BIT_Office client with 8 devices, 2 LLDP neighbors, 2 MAC connections
context_for_next_testing_agent: Enterprise topology feature fully validated. Backend: GET /api/network/topology/{client_id} returns 6-layer hierarchy with LLDP edges (source=lldp), MAC edges (source=mac_table with 10G labels), and inferred edges (10G Uplink for switches with 10G in name). Frontend: React Flow map shows 9 nodes with hierarchical layout, LLDP/MAC badges, toolbar buttons (Modifica, Auto, Reset, Salva Layout) all functional.
